features.name: oversized hex literal assigned to signal without truncation warning
features.description: Code should include a sized hex literal with explicit width prefix that has significantly more bits than the target signal width, where the assignment silently truncates without warning

features.name: increment overflow on oversized literal addition
features.description: Code should include an addition operation where a signal is incremented by an oversized hex literal that gets silently truncated, causing incorrect wraparound behavior
features.snippet:
logic [128:0] cnt;
always @(posedge clk) cnt = cnt + 129'hdeadbeefc001f00ddeadbeefc001f00ddeadbeefc001f00d;
